global_context.scene_description: A bold editorial cartoon-illustrated split-frame composition, single image with a clean vertical divider down the middle. Same homeowner character drawn in both frames — recognizable, expressive, slightly exaggerated cartoon proportions. Theatrical lighting with strong color blocks behind each frame. Editorial illustration style, NOT photorealistic, NOT flat infographic — closer to New Yorker editorial cartoon meets modern brand illustration. LEFT FRAME (cool blue-grey background, slightly desaturated, anxious mood): the homeowner is mid-stride walking toward a door, body tense, shoulders hunched, sweat drops on his forehead, eyebrows pinched together, mouth a tight line, hands clenched into nervous fists. A large rounded speech bubble emerges from his head reading exactly: 'This is gonna be $20K and they'll pressure me into upgrades...' RIGHT FRAME (warm sunlit background, relieved mood): the SAME homeowner walking the opposite direction, away from the door, posture relaxed, shoulders dropped, a folded quote/document in his hand, eyebrows raised in pleasant surprise, the corner of his mouth turned up in a small disbelieving smile. A speech bubble from his head reads exactly: 'Wait... that's it?' A bold black vertical line separates the two halves. Above the entire image, a clean banner-style headline reads in bold uppercase: 'WHAT EVERY PHOENIX HOMEOWNER EXPECTS. WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENS.' At the bottom, a small italic caption: 'New AC install. It's not what you've been telling yourself.' Mood: the satisfying click of a wrong assumption being corrected. Editorial cartoon illustration, theatrical color blocking, expressive character work.

lighting.quality: left frame cool/anxious, right frame warm/relieved

composition.framing: vertical split-frame, two equal halves divided by a bold vertical line, headline banner across top, small caption strip across bottom
composition.focal_point: the contrast between the tense character on the left and the relaxed character on the right
